title: Marriage Preparation Programme for Minor and Young Muslim Couples
text:
Marriage Preparation Programme for Minor and Young Muslim Couples - SupportGoWhere
Marriage Preparation Programme for Minor and Young Muslim Couples
Share link
Provides pre- and post-marriage support for minor and young couples.
SERVICE HIGHLIGHTS
Centre-based
Free
Referral not required
SERVICE PROVIDERS
Find service providers near you
Collapse all sections
About this support

Supports minor and young couples through premarital consultation session, marriage preparation workshops, and parents support group.

Who is this for?

Minor Couples (Mandatory to complete Marriage Preparation Programme before applying for marriage):

Either party is below 21 years old

At least one party is Singapore Citizen or Permanent Resident

Both bride and groom are Muslims

Young Couples:

Groom is between 21 and 24 years old and bride is aged 21 years and above

At least one party is Singapore Citizen or Permanent Resident

Both bride and groom are Muslims

What to expect?

For minor couples

Sign up (email or call) directly with the agency for the mandatory (requried by law) complimentary Marriage Preparation Programme (MPP) before proceeding with marriage application via Registry of Muslim Marriages (ROMM) website.

Upon completion of MPP, the agency will provide casework and counselling if necessary for up to 10 years and refer couples with higher risks to FSC or SSO.

Eligible couples will also be auto-referred to KidStart. Those who prefer not to be auto-referred, can complete an opt out form instead for the agency to follow-up with them.

For young couples

Similar as above, except that the MPP is not mandatory for young couples.

They can apply e-file for marriage (OMJ) before attending MPP.

Upon completion of the MPP, the other services offered to minor couples are applicable to young couples too.

How to apply?

Call, email, visit or through referrals by other agencies to apply.

Information last updated on 30 Sep 2025
